FALQON discretised feedback law sets beta_{k+1} = -<i[H_d,H_p]>_k: a Lyapunov control that drives the energy monotonically downward because the sign of the update opposes the gradient, guaranteeing the energy decreases or stays flat from layer to layer. Each layer must measure the commutator expectation <i[H_d,H_p]>.

Fault-tolerant cost here means: logical versus physical qubits, T-gate count, and wall-clock time via a surface-code layer (physical error rate against threshold, code-cycle time, magic-state distillation). These are the units every later number is quoted in.

The feedback estimate carries a precision eps_fb, and the coherent register width is b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb)) + 2 bits, forced by that precision. Circuit depth will be shown to scale against this precision - not against a convergence criterion.

Amplitude estimation at Heisenberg scaling reduces the per-layer shot overhead but leaves the classical measurement-and-restart loop intact, so the quadratic layer structure persists and the wall clock falls only to 3.2 yr .
**Fully coherent feedback.** The structural bottleneck of the two preceding strategies is the classical loop: a measurement collapses the state, obligating a restart.

json.essence: C_{\text{SQL}}(L,\epsilon_{fb}) \;\sim\; \frac{L^2}{2}\cdot\frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}^2} \qquad (L\to\infty). \label{eq:sql-result}

json.ctx: estimation of an expectation value to additive precision $\epsilon_{fb}$ is a mean-estimation problem governed by the central limit theorem: the standard error of the sample mean over $N$ shots scales as $\sigma/\sqrt{N}$ for bounded-variance observables, so achieving error $\epsilon_{fb}$ requires
json.essence: N_{\text{shots}}(\epsilon_{fb}) \sim \frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}^2}. \label{eq:sql-shots}

json.ctx: a_{k+1}$ is \begin{equation} C_k = k \cdot N_{\text{shots}}(\epsilon_{fb}) = \frac{k}{\epsilon_{fb}^2}. \label{eq:sql-ck} \end{equation} \medskip \noindent\textbf{Step 4 (sum over the whole run).} The full run performs this estimate once per layer, $k = 1,\dots,L$. The total layer-execution count is
json.essence: C_{\text{SQL}}(L,\epsilon_{fb}) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} C_k = \frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}^2}\sum_{k=1}^{L} k = \frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}^2}\cdot\frac{L(L+1)}{2}. \label{eq:sql-sum}

json.ctx: ^3) \\ &= +\frac{dt^2}{2}[A,B] + O(dt^3). \label{eq:baerr} \end{split} \end{equation} The two orderings' leading error terms are exactly equal in magnitude and opposite in sign. \paragraph{Step 5 --- the symmetrised (quiet-branch) combination.} Averaging Eqs.~\eqref{eq:bareerr} and \eqref{eq:baerr},
json.essence: \begin{split} \frac{U_{AB}+U_{BA}}{2} &- e^{-i(A+B)dt} \\ &= \frac{1}{2}\left[-\frac{dt^2}{2}[A,B] + \frac{dt^2}{2}[A,B]\right] + O(dt^3) \\ &= O(dt^3). \label{eq:symerr} \end{split}

json.ctx: is a separate loop not removed here.} \medskip \noindent\textbf{Step 1 (the ADAPT-VQE loop as a measure-driven iteration).} ADAPT-VQE~grows an ansatz one operator at a time from a fixed pool of anti-Hermitian generators $\{A_i\}_{i=1}^{P}$. After macro-iteration $k$ the state is a depth-$k$ product
json.essence: \lvert\psi_k\rangle = \prod_{j=1}^{k} e^{\theta_j A_{i_j}} \lvert\mathrm{ref}\rangle . \label{eq:vqe-ansatz}

json.ctx: A_i]^\dagger=[A_i^\dagger,H^\dagger]=[-A_i,H]=[H,A_i]$, so $[H,A_i]$ is Hermitian. This is the same shape as FALQON's $A_k=\langle i[H_d,H_p]\rangle_k$ (a commutator expectation), evaluated once \emph{per pool element} rather than once per layer. The round-$(k{+}1)$ unitary is fixed by the selection
json.essence: i^{\star} = \operatorname*{arg\,max}_{1\le i\le P} \lvert g^{(k)}_i\rvert, \qquad \text{append } e^{\theta_{i^\star} A_{i^\star}} . \label{eq:vqe-argmax}

json.ctx: a depth-$k$ product \begin{equation} \lvert\psi_k\rangle = \prod_{j=1}^{k} e^{\theta_j A_{i_j}} \lvert\mathrm{ref}\rangle . \label{eq:vqe-ansatz} \end{equation} The next operator is chosen by the energy gradient with respect to prepending each pool element. For $A_i^\dagger=-A_i$ and $H^\dagger=H$,
json.essence: g^{(k)}_i \;=\; \left.\frac{\partial}{\partial\theta}\right|_{\theta=0}\!\langle\psi_k\rvert e^{-\theta A_i} H\, e^{\theta A_i}\lvert\psi_k\rangle \;=\; \langle\psi_k\rvert [H,A_i]\lvert\psi_k\rangle , \label{eq:vqe-grad}

json.ctx: emize} \medskip \noindent\textbf{Step 4 (collapse in $L$, with the pool prefactor named).} Under C1--C3 each macro-iteration is one coherent pass: coherent-write $g^{(k)}$, run $\mathcal{C}$, apply $\textsc{sel}(\theta_{i^\star})$, uncompute. There is no collapse, so rounds $1,\dots,L$ execute once:
json.essence: C_{\text{coherent}}(L) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} c_P = c_P\,L = O(P\,L), \quad c_P = O(P b), \label{eq:vqe-linear}

json.ctx: needs $\sim 1/\epsilon^2$ shots (shot-noise mean estimation, Eq.~\eqref{eq:vqe-grad} is a bounded Hermitian observable), and each shot re-prepares the depth-$k$ state of Eq.~\eqref{eq:vqe-ansatz} from the reference (measurement collapses $\lvert\psi_k\rangle$). Summing over the $L$ macro-iterations,
json.essence: C_{\text{measured}}(L,\epsilon) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} \frac{P\,k}{\epsilon^2} = \frac{P\,L(L+1)}{2\epsilon^2} \;\sim\; \frac{P\,L^2}{2\epsilon^2}, \label{eq:vqe-quadratic}

json.ctx: e $g^{(k)}$, run $\mathcal{C}$, apply $\textsc{sel}(\theta_{i^\star})$, uncompute. There is no collapse, so rounds $1,\dots,L$ execute once: \begin{equation} C_{\text{coherent}}(L) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} c_P = c_P\,L = O(P\,L), \quad c_P = O(P b), \label{eq:vqe-linear} \end{equation} and the separation is
json.essence: \frac{C_{\text{measured}}}{C_{\text{coherent}}} \;\sim\; \frac{P\,L^2/(2\epsilon^2)}{c_P\,L} \;=\; \frac{L}{2 c'\epsilon^2}, \ c' = c_P/P = O(b), \label{eq:vqe-ratio}

json.ctx: versible comparator/argmax network $\mathcal{C}$ writing the winning index into an $\lceil\log_2 P\rceil$-bit index register, $\mathcal{C}:\lvert g^{(k)}\rangle\lvert 0\rangle_{\mathrm{idx}}\mapsto\lvert g^{(k)}\rangle\lvert i^{\star}\rangle$, followed by a \textsc{select} multiplexer over the pool,
json.essence: \textsc{sel}(\theta) = \sum_{i=1}^{P} \lvert i\rangle\!\langle i\rvert_{\mathrm{idx}} \otimes e^{\theta A_i}, \label{eq:vqe-select}

json.ctx: whole run, written coherently each layer and consumed by the register-controlled driver. The witness's single ancilla in \eqref{eq:wf-ledger} is drawn from — or added to — this pre-existing coherent register rather than requiring a new one to be prepared, threaded, and later discarded from scratch:
json.essence: \Delta(\text{ancilla count}) = 0 \label{eq:wf-anc}

json.ctx: s the host loop's $O(L)$ scaling class untouched.} \medskip \noindent\textbf{Step 1 (per-checkpoint cost).} Inserted between two chosen FALQON layers, the two-ordering witness (the $U_{AB}$/$U_{BA}$ interferometer, derived above) costs, under the surface-code / Clifford+T accounting used throughout:
json.essence: \text{(ancilla)} = 1, \; \text{(rotation depth)} \approx d_{\text{layer}}, \; \text{(readout)} = 1, \label{eq:wf-ledger}

json.ctx: r. \medskip \noindent\textbf{Step 3 (amortised fractional depth overhead).} Let checkpoints be placed every $m$ layers ($m\ge 1$). Between two checkpoints the host loop executes $m$ layers at depth $d_{\text{layer}}$ each, i.e.\ $m\,d_{\text{layer}}$, to which one checkpoint adds $d_{\text{layer}}$:
json.essence: \text{overhead fraction} = \frac{d_{\text{layer}}}{m \cdot d_{\text{layer}}} = \frac{1}{m}. \label{eq:wf-overhead}

json.ctx: edback register (Step 2) nor forces re-execution from layer $1$ (Step 4), the host loop's per-layer cost structure established for the coherent branch — $C_{\text{coherent}}(L) = cL$, the linear result derived above --- is unmodified by the insertion of checkpoints; only the constant $c$ is shifted:
json.essence: \begin{split} C_{\text{coherent+witness}}(L) &= c\,L + \frac{L}{m}\,d_{\text{layer}} \\ &= \left(c + \frac{d_{\text{layer}}}{m}\right) L = c' L = O(L). \end{split} \label{eq:wf-total}

The fault-tolerant cost of FALQON's per-layer feedback measurement — how the measurement-and-restart structure and the required precision eps_fb scale against circuit depth — is the question a fault-tolerant resource analysis of FALQON must address.
The problem instance is a 30-site 2D Fermi-Hubbard model on a 6x5 lattice, two spin species per site, encoded on 60 logical qubits via Jordan-Wigner (one qubit per spin-orbital).
The 6x5 lattice has 49 nearest-neighbour edges, giving 98 hopping terms (2 spins per bond) and 30 on-site interaction terms.

One first-order Trotter step costs 226 Rz rotations; the second-order step is modelled at 1.5x, giving 339 Rz per step under a fermionic swap-network locality assumption, with one step per FALQON layer and a Trotter depth of 339 logical cycles per layer.
Each Rz synthesises to 100 T gates at per-rotation synthesis error 1e-10 (Ross-Selinger class, ~3 log2(1/eps) T per rotation), so one Trotter step costs 33,900 T gates per layer.
FALQON's feedback law beta_{k+1} = -<i[H_d,H_p]>_k realises Lyapunov monotonic descent of <H_p> layer to layer, with no classical optimizer consulting the parameter history.

Under SQL (shot-sampling) feedback each layer re-executes all preceding layers (L^2/2 structure at 1/eps^2 shots per layer); at L=500, eps_fb=0.01 the run needs 61 logical qubits, code distance 31, 235,863 physical qubits, 4.2375e14 total T gates, wall clock 2,502.1 yr.

Amplitude estimation at Heisenberg scaling ((pi/2)/eps_fb controlled applications) reduces per-layer shot overhead but keeps the classical measure-and-restart loop, so the L^2 structure persists; at L=500, eps_fb=0.01: 69 logical qubits, code distance 25, 189,375 physical qubits, 6.656e11 T gates, wall clock 3.2 yr.

Fully coherent feedback writes A_k into a 9-bit ancilla register without measurement and applies the driver controlled on that register; no re-preparation is required, circuit volume scales as L not L^2; at L=500, eps_fb=0.01: 80 logical qubits, code distance 21, 165,840 physical qubits, 5.342e9 T gates, wall clock 187.0 h.
The coherent register width is b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb)) + 2 = 9 bits at eps_fb = 0.01, with a factor-2 overhead for the register-controlled driver evolution and uncompute.

COHERENT wall clock scales linearly in layer count at eps_fb=0.01: 33.8 h at 100 layers (d=19, 146,640 physical qubits), 187.0 h at 500 (d=21, 165,840), 819.1 h at 2000 (d=23, 186,960); the logical register stays fixed at 80 qubits.

Feedback-precision sweep at L=500: SQL wall clock is 21.8 yr (eps 0.1), 2,502.1 yr (0.01), 282,500.0 yr (0.001) — eps^-2 scaling; COHERENT is 17.4 h, 187.0 h, 85.1 d — eps^-1 scaling; wall factors ~13,000 (SQL) vs ~120 (COHERENT) over the range.

Factory count is a pure throughput multiplier: at L=500, eps_fb=0.01 the COHERENT wall clock is 77.9 d at 1 factory (111,840 physical qubits), 187.0 h at 10 (165,840), 18.7 h at 100 (705,840); the total T-gate count is 5.342e9 in every case.

T-count ratios at the headline point: SQL/AE = 640, AE/COHERENT = 120, SQL/COHERENT = 79,000 (~5 orders of magnitude); wall-clock SQL/COHERENT = 120,000 (~4 orders; throughput from the lower code distance partially offsets the T-count ratio).

The two-ordering witness: control U_AB on ancilla 0 and U_BA on ancilla 1, Hadamard, measure; P(flag) = (1/2)(1 - Re <U_BA^dag U_AB>); dark exactly when [A,B]=0; flag probability scales as dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 to leading order; the quiet branch is the symmetrised step accurate to O(dt^3) (single-step analogue of multiproduct formulas); the flag branch isolates the error term.
Dark case (2-qubit TFIM at g=0, commuting layers): P(flag) = 0.0 and quiet-branch disturbance 0.0, exactly.
Flag scaling: the log-log slope of P(flag) vs dt is 3.949 (theory: 4) across dt in {0.4, 0.2, 0.1, 0.05, 0.025}, converging onto dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 to better than one part in a thousand by dt=0.025.

Error cancellation: bare first-order Trotter infidelity scales as dt^3.951 while the quiet branch scales as dt^5.907 (~two orders of accuracy gained); at dt=0.4 the bare infidelity is 0.033 vs quiet-branch 4.6e-4.
Full evolution over T=2.0 in 40 steps: plain Trotter infidelity 1.18e-4 vs all-quiet witnessed 5.2e-6, with cumulative acceptance probability 0.9996.
Large-error detection: at dt=0.8 the flag fires with probability 0.341 — appreciable errors are reliably detected rather than silently accepted.

The witness inserts between FALQON layers at chosen checkpoints at the cost of one ancilla, roughly the depth of a second step, and a single computational-basis readout, without breaking the coherent run; the quiet outcome continues the run on the error-improved symmetrised step.

Against QPE-class estimates the comparison is qualitative only: published Trotter-QPE, early-fault-tolerant, and qubitization estimates for Hubbard-class systems reach wall clocks into decades under standard T-factory throughput; the output types differ — coherent-FALQON delivers a prepared state, QPE an energy.
A drafter arithmetic error (sweep ratio stated as 50; the model's emitted value is 120) was caught mechanically by the numeral gate, not a judge.

A confabulated section-9 derivation (a Kraus-operator argument fusing the witness commutator with the feedback commutator, claiming the feedback register doubles as the witness at no gate cost) was unsupported by any source and rewritten to the supported insertion claim.
An overclaim in section 8 ("matches to within numerical precision at every step size") was false at dt=0.4 (15% higher-order gap; 0.06% by dt=0.025) and was restated as convergence.
A misattribution in section 7 (SQL described as amplitude estimation; it is shot sampling) was fixed, and an uncited textbook name-drop neutralized to a generic textbook reference.

The weld pass wrote over its input file; the claim-lock diff ran against a deterministic rebuild of the pre-weld object — verified: 31 cite instances, 143 source tags, numeral multiset identical except one extra occurrence of the ledger-sourced 30 introduced by a seam bridge.
The section-9 rewrite removed two citation instances (`magann2022lyapunov` and `childs2021theory` at that location); both keys remain live elsewhere in the paper.

`childs2012hamiltonian` anchors amplitude-estimation scaling in section 4 but is the adjacent LCU paper, not the canonical AE reference; replace with a Brassard-class citation once fetched through the door.
All 11 literature records were constructed without fetch access and are marked trust=unverified pending a door-check; their contents as cited are claims to be confirmed, not established fact.

The TO-VERIFY constants bound how far the headline numbers should be trusted: the rotation count vs Kivlichan swap-network tables (`kivlichan2020improved`), the factory footprint and rate vs Litinski (`litinski2019game`), and the Ross-Selinger constant (`ross2016optimal`).
The commuting-group decomposition of i[H_d,H_p] for the Hubbard driver (measurement groups = 10, SCENARIO) needs the explicit construction; no source file provides it.
The coherent register arithmetic is coarse: the 2x compute/uncompute overhead is a placeholder and the register-controlled driver costs ~b controlled evolutions; a proper costing is v2 work.

The quantitative QPE comparison is deferred until the cited papers' numbers (`kivlichan2020improved`, `campbell2021early`, `babbush2018encoding`) enter through the door; V1 states only the qualitative scale claim.

FALQON convergence at 60 logical qubits is unmeasured; the 500-layer assumption is the scenario's largest unverified input, and whether FALQON converges at this scale at all is an open empirical question.

Figures (the sweep tradeoff plot and the witness scaling plot, from the readings JSONs) are registered TODOs with none rendered; the large-error flag-branch fidelity 0.178 is present in the data but undiscussed in V1.

cites.essence: Lyapunov-control-inspired strategies for quantum combinatorial optimization. The continuous-time Lyapunov feedback derivation: choosing beta(t) = -<i[H_d,H_p]> makes d<H_p>/dt non-positive by construction, the descent guarantee FALQON discretizes.

Theory of Trotter Error with Commutator Scaling. Trotter error controlled by nested commutators: the theoretical basis for both the per-step error discussion and the witness's dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 flag-probability scaling.

Hamiltonian Simulation Using Linear Combinations of Unitary Operations. Linear combinations of unitaries: the witness's controlled ordering is a two-term LCU over the ordering index. Also used in section 4 as the amplitude-estimation scaling anchor — flagged in the drift ledger as adjacent, not canonical (replace with a Brassard-class AE citation at the door-check).

Improved Fault-Tolerant Quantum Simulation of Condensed-Phase Correlated Electrons via Trotterization. Fermionic swap-network Trotterization for the Hubbard model: the locality assumption under the 339-rotation-per-step count (TO-VERIFY against its tables), and a member of the QPE-class comparison set.

A Game of Surface Codes: Large-Scale Quantum Computing with Lattice Surgery. Lattice-surgery layouts and 15-to-1 magic-state distillation: the closest allowed anchor for the factory footprint (6000 physical qubits) and rate (60 cycles per T) — link inferential, TO-VERIFY.

Optimal Ancilla-Free Clifford+T Approximation of Z-Rotations. Rz-to-Clifford+T synthesis at ~3 log2(1/eps) T gates per rotation: the source of the 100-T-per-rotation figure at synthesis error 1e-10 (constant TO-VERIFY).

Early fault-tolerant simulations of the Hubbard model. Early-fault-tolerant Hubbard-model simulation estimates: a member of the QPE-class comparison set against which the coherent-FALQON figures are qualitatively positioned.

Encoding Electronic Spectra in Quantum Circuits with Linear T Complexity. Qubitization/LCU linear-T-complexity encoding: anchor for the register-and-workspace qubit overhead of the coherent strategy, and a member of the QPE-class comparison set.

json.essence: 9. The witness inside the coherent run, and the honest limits

spine.essence: 9. The witness inside the coherent run, and the honest limits
spine.narrative: That the witness needs no ancilla an LCU would not already carry has a practical consequence inside the coherent run: the witness inserts between layers without breaking the run.

json.essence: A broad family of quantum algorithms chooses each next operation from a measurement of the current state: the algorithm measures an observable, and the outcome sets the next unitary. These are the feedback, or adaptive, algorithms - introduced here by example before any instance is named formally.

Each measurement collapses the state, so the computation must restart from the beginning to produce the next layer. Cost therefore compounds with the number of iterations: the measure-and-restart structure is the object under study.

FALQON (Lyapunov feedback control) is the concrete representative of the family whose cost this paper estimates: at each layer it measures the feedback observable i[H_d,H_p] and sets the next driver coefficient from it. Adaptive VQE, iterative phase estimation, and error-adaptive compilation are siblings in the same measurement-driven family.

Existing resource estimates price these algorithms as fixed circuits. The measurement bottleneck that dominates their asymptotic cost - and whether it can be removed - has not been costed as a scaling class.

This work shows that confining measurement to one heralding bit moves the whole class from the measurement-dominated regime (millennia) to the coherence-dominated one (hours): circuit volume collapses L^2 to L, and the regime boundary is a property of the algorithm class, not the instance. Two results carry it - the coherent-feedback scaling collapse, and the mid-circuit Trotter witness - the two halves of one thesis.

The argument proceeds: fix the instance and its assumptions; give FALQON measured quantity; set the Trotter step; compare the three evaluation routes; report the estimates; identify the levers; position against the QPE-class literature; present the mid-circuit witness; state the honest limits.

FALQON discretised feedback law sets beta_{k+1} = -<i[H_d,H_p]>_k: a Lyapunov control that drives the energy monotonically downward because the sign of the update opposes the gradient, guaranteeing the energy decreases or stays flat from layer to layer. Each layer must measure the commutator expectation <i[H_d,H_p]>.

Fault-tolerant cost here means: logical versus physical qubits, T-gate count, and wall-clock time via a surface-code layer (physical error rate against threshold, code-cycle time, magic-state distillation). These are the units every later number is quoted in.

The feedback estimate carries a precision eps_fb, and the coherent register width is b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb)) + 2 bits, forced by that precision. Circuit depth will be shown to scale against this precision - not against a convergence criterion.

Amplitude estimation at Heisenberg scaling reduces the per-layer shot overhead but leaves the classical measurement-and-restart loop intact, so the quadratic layer structure persists and the wall clock falls only to 3.2 yr .
**Fully coherent feedback.** The structural bottleneck of the two preceding strategies is the classical loop: a measurement collapses the state, obligating a restart.

N_costs + N_quiet: per-layer resource ledger (s3) + the quiet symmetrised step is derived to N_free: witness rides inside the coherent feedback loop at ONE heralding bit, no restart.

N_prec: feedback precision eps_fb=0.01 (s4) is derived to N_regwidth: coherent register width b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb))+2 = 9 bits.
N_readings: the manuscript's emitted resource numbers (s4/s5/s6) is computed to N_ratios: SQL/COHERENT ~79,000 (T-count) / ~120,000 (wall-clock), 5 orders, across L=100..2000.
N_regwidth: 9-bit coherent register + register-controlled driver (s4) is derived to N_coherent_l: coherent circuit volume ~ L (single pass, constant control overhead).

N_separation: the measure-and-restart L^2->L mechanism is derived to N_generality: the collapse is FALQON/Hubbard-INDEPENDENT — general to measurement-driven iterative algorithms with a coherently expressible feedback observable.
N_sql_l2 + N_coherent_l: the two scalings side by side is derived to N_separation: SQL/COHERENT gap is a scaling-CLASS separation, ratio ~ L (widens), not a constant.

The commutator term that appears in and with opposite signs cancels in their sum, so the symmetrised step is accurate to rather than : detection and leading-error cancellation occur in the same measurement.
The comparison to QPE-class estimates is therefore not apples-to-apples in output type.

The per-layer cost is proportional to for the amplitude estimation plus a constant overhead for the register-controlled driver evolution and uncompute , giving a total layer-execution count that scales as rather than .
The physical qubit count is thus modestly layer-dependent, growing with code distance as the T-count rises, while the logical register remains fixed at 80 qubits across all three layer counts .
The readout does not collapse the system register the way the classical feedback loop does.

The fault-tolerant cost of FALQON's per-layer feedback measurement — how the measurement-and-restart structure and the required precision eps_fb scale against circuit depth — is the question a fault-tolerant resource analysis of FALQON must address.
The problem instance is a 30-site 2D Fermi-Hubbard model on a 6x5 lattice, two spin species per site, encoded on 60 logical qubits via Jordan-Wigner (one qubit per spin-orbital).
system_qubits: Model parameter system_qubits = 60 logical qubits. (Model tier DERIVED; one qubit per spin-orbital, Jordan-Wigner.)
lattice_y: Model parameter lattice_y = 5 sites. (Model tier SCENARIO.)
lattice_x: Model parameter lattice_x = 6 sites. (Model tier SCENARIO; 30-site 2D Hubbard as 6x5.)
The 6x5 lattice has 49 nearest-neighbour edges, giving 98 hopping terms (2 spins per bond) and 30 on-site interaction terms.
onsite_terms: Model parameter onsite_terms = 30 terms. (Model tier DERIVED; U n_up n_down per site.)
hopping_terms: Model parameter hopping_terms = 98 terms. (Model tier DERIVED; 2 spins per bond.)
lattice_edges: Model parameter lattice_edges = 49 edges. (Model tier DERIVED; nearest-neighbour bonds of the 6x5 lattice.)

One first-order Trotter step costs 226 Rz rotations; the second-order step is modelled at 1.5x, giving 339 Rz per step under a fermionic swap-network locality assumption, with one step per FALQON layer and a Trotter depth of 339 logical cycles per layer.
falqon_layers: Model parameter falqon_layers = 500 layers. (Model tier SCENARIO; convergence is THE open empirical question at this scale; swept 100-2000.)
trotter_steps_per_layer: Model parameter trotter_steps_per_layer = 1 step. (Model tier SCENARIO; FALQON takes dt small, one 2nd-order step per layer.)

rotations_per_trotter_step: Model parameter rotations_per_trotter_step = 339 Rz. (Model tier DERIVED; 2nd-order step ~1.5x first-order count; swap-network locality assumed, TO-VERIFY vs Kivlichan-class counts at the door.)
first_order_rotations: Model parameter first_order_rotations = 226 Rz. (Model tier DERIVED; one rotation per hopping pair plus one per on-site term.)
Each Rz synthesises to 100 T gates at per-rotation synthesis error 1e-10 (Ross-Selinger class, ~3 log2(1/eps) T per rotation), so one Trotter step costs 33,900 T gates per layer.
rotation_synthesis_error: Model parameter rotation_synthesis_error = 1e-10. (Model tier SCENARIO; per-rotation synthesis target, budget-consistent with total rotation count.)
T_per_rotation: Model parameter T_per_rotation = 100 T gates. (Model tier LITERATURE (ross-selinger-class TO-VERIFY); ~3 log2(1/eps) T per Rz.)
FALQON's feedback law beta_{k+1} = -<i[H_d,H_p]>_k realises Lyapunov monotonic descent of <H_p> layer to layer, with no classical optimizer consulting the parameter history.

Under SQL (shot-sampling) feedback each layer re-executes all preceding layers (L^2/2 structure at 1/eps^2 shots per layer); at L=500, eps_fb=0.01 the run needs 61 logical qubits, code distance 31, 235,863 physical qubits, 4.2375e14 total T gates, wall clock 2,502.1 yr.

Amplitude estimation at Heisenberg scaling ((pi/2)/eps_fb controlled applications) reduces per-layer shot overhead but keeps the classical measure-and-restart loop, so the L^2 structure persists; at L=500, eps_fb=0.01: 69 logical qubits, code distance 25, 189,375 physical qubits, 6.656e11 T gates, wall clock 3.2 yr.
amplitude_estimation_constant: Model parameter amplitude_estimation_constant = 1.5708. (Model tier TEXTBOOK; ~(pi/2)/eps controlled applications, Heisenberg-limited.)

Fully coherent feedback writes A_k into a 9-bit ancilla register without measurement and applies the driver controlled on that register; no re-preparation is required, circuit volume scales as L not L^2; at L=500, eps_fb=0.01: 80 logical qubits, code distance 21, 165,840 physical qubits, 5.342e9 T gates, wall clock 187.0 h.
The coherent register width is b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb)) + 2 = 9 bits at eps_fb = 0.01, with a factor-2 overhead for the register-controlled driver evolution and uncompute.
coherent_ctrl_overhead: Model parameter coherent_ctrl_overhead = 2. (Model tier SCENARIO; register-controlled driver evolution + uncompute vs bare layer (see FAL-CLM-211).)
coherent_register_bits: Model parameter coherent_register_bits = 9 logical qubits. (Model tier DERIVED; b = log2(1/eps_fb)+2 bits hold A_k coherently.)

COHERENT wall clock scales linearly in layer count at eps_fb=0.01: 33.8 h at 100 layers (d=19, 146,640 physical qubits), 187.0 h at 500 (d=21, 165,840), 819.1 h at 2000 (d=23, 186,960); the logical register stays fixed at 80 qubits.

Feedback-precision sweep at L=500: SQL wall clock is 21.8 yr (eps 0.1), 2,502.1 yr (0.01), 282,500.0 yr (0.001) — eps^-2 scaling; COHERENT is 17.4 h, 187.0 h, 85.1 d — eps^-1 scaling; wall factors ~13,000 (SQL) vs ~120 (COHERENT) over the range.
feedback_precision: Model parameter feedback_precision = 0.01. (Model tier SCENARIO; per-layer precision on A_k; swept 1e-1..1e-3.)

Factory count is a pure throughput multiplier: at L=500, eps_fb=0.01 the COHERENT wall clock is 77.9 d at 1 factory (111,840 physical qubits), 187.0 h at 10 (165,840), 18.7 h at 100 (705,840); the total T-gate count is 5.342e9 in every case.

t_factory_physical_qubits: Model parameter t_factory_physical_qubits = 6000 physical qubits. (Model tier LITERATURE (15-to-1 distillation-class TO-VERIFY); citekey link inferential (V1 section 1 friction).)
n_t_factories: Model parameter n_t_factories = 10 factories. (Model tier SCENARIO; swept 1-100.)

T-count ratios at the headline point: SQL/AE = 640, AE/COHERENT = 120, SQL/COHERENT = 79,000 (~5 orders of magnitude); wall-clock SQL/COHERENT = 120,000 (~4 orders; throughput from the lower code distance partially offsets the T-count ratio).

The two-ordering witness: control U_AB on ancilla 0 and U_BA on ancilla 1, Hadamard, measure; P(flag) = (1/2)(1 - Re <U_BA^dag U_AB>); dark exactly when [A,B]=0; flag probability scales as dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 to leading order; the quiet branch is the symmetrised step accurate to O(dt^3) (single-step analogue of multiproduct formulas); the flag branch isolates the error term.
Dark case (2-qubit TFIM at g=0, commuting layers): P(flag) = 0.0 and quiet-branch disturbance 0.0, exactly.
Flag scaling: the log-log slope of P(flag) vs dt is 3.949 (theory: 4) across dt in {0.4, 0.2, 0.1, 0.05, 0.025}, converging onto dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 to better than one part in a thousand by dt=0.025.

Error cancellation: bare first-order Trotter infidelity scales as dt^3.951 while the quiet branch scales as dt^5.907 (~two orders of accuracy gained); at dt=0.4 the bare infidelity is 0.033 vs quiet-branch 4.6e-4.
Full evolution over T=2.0 in 40 steps: plain Trotter infidelity 1.18e-4 vs all-quiet witnessed 5.2e-6, with cumulative acceptance probability 0.9996.
Large-error detection: at dt=0.8 the flag fires with probability 0.341 — appreciable errors are reliably detected rather than silently accepted.

The witness inserts between FALQON layers at chosen checkpoints at the cost of one ancilla, roughly the depth of a second step, and a single computational-basis readout, without breaking the coherent run; the quiet outcome continues the run on the error-improved symmetrised step.
falqon_layers: Model parameter falqon_layers = 500 layers. (Model tier SCENARIO; convergence is THE open empirical question at this scale; swept 100-2000.)

Against QPE-class estimates the comparison is qualitative only: published Trotter-QPE, early-fault-tolerant, and qubitization estimates for Hubbard-class systems reach wall clocks into decades under standard T-factory throughput; the output types differ — coherent-FALQON delivers a prepared state, QPE an energy.
A drafter arithmetic error (sweep ratio stated as 50; the model's emitted value is 120) was caught mechanically by the numeral gate, not a judge.

A confabulated section-9 derivation (a Kraus-operator argument fusing the witness commutator with the feedback commutator, claiming the feedback register doubles as the witness at no gate cost) was unsupported by any source and rewritten to the supported insertion claim.
An overclaim in section 8 ("matches to within numerical precision at every step size") was false at dt=0.4 (15% higher-order gap; 0.06% by dt=0.025) and was restated as convergence.
A misattribution in section 7 (SQL described as amplitude estimation; it is shot sampling) was fixed, and an uncited textbook name-drop neutralized to a generic textbook reference.

Background
Introduction
1. The setting, and every assumption on the table
2. FALQON, and what it must measure
3. The Trotter step for the 6x5 Hubbard lattice
4. Three ways to evaluate the feedback law
5. The estimates
6. What moves the numbers
7. Against the QPE-class estimates
8. Detecting Trotter error mid-circuit: the two-ordering witness
9. The witness inside the coherent run, and the honest limits

A broad family of quantum algorithms chooses each next operation from a measurement of the current state: the algorithm measures an observable, and the outcome sets the next unitary. These are the feedback, or adaptive, algorithms - introduced here by example before any instance is named formally.

Each measurement collapses the state, so the computation must restart from the beginning to produce the next layer. Cost therefore compounds with the number of iterations: the measure-and-restart structure is the object under study.

FALQON (Lyapunov feedback control) is the concrete representative of the family whose cost this paper estimates: at each layer it measures the feedback observable i[H_d,H_p] and sets the next driver coefficient from it. Adaptive VQE, iterative phase estimation, and error-adaptive compilation are siblings in the same measurement-driven family.

Existing resource estimates price these algorithms as fixed circuits. The measurement bottleneck that dominates their asymptotic cost - and whether it can be removed - has not been costed as a scaling class.

This work shows that confining measurement to one heralding bit moves the whole class from the measurement-dominated regime (millennia) to the coherence-dominated one (hours): circuit volume collapses L^2 to L, and the regime boundary is a property of the algorithm class, not the instance. Two results carry it - the coherent-feedback scaling collapse, and the mid-circuit Trotter witness - the two halves of one thesis.

The argument proceeds: fix the instance and its assumptions; give FALQON measured quantity; set the Trotter step; compare the three evaluation routes; report the estimates; identify the levers; position against the QPE-class literature; present the mid-circuit witness; state the honest limits.

records.essence: The feedback estimate carries a precision eps_fb, and the coherent register width is b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb)) + 2 bits, forced by that precision. Circuit depth will be shown to scale against this precision - not against a convergence criterion.

Fault-tolerant cost here means: logical versus physical qubits, T-gate count, and wall-clock time via a surface-code layer (physical error rate against threshold, code-cycle time, magic-state distillation). These are the units every later number is quoted in.

FALQON discretised feedback law sets beta_{k+1} = -<i[H_d,H_p]>_k: a Lyapunov control that drives the energy monotonically downward because the sign of the update opposes the gradient, guaranteeing the energy decreases or stays flat from layer to layer. Each layer must measure the commutator expectation <i[H_d,H_p]>.

The argument proceeds: fix the instance and its assumptions; give FALQON measured quantity; set the Trotter step; compare the three evaluation routes; report the estimates; identify the levers; position against the QPE-class literature; present the mid-circuit witness; state the honest limits.

This work shows that confining measurement to one heralding bit moves the whole class from the measurement-dominated regime (millennia) to the coherence-dominated one (hours): circuit volume collapses L^2 to L, and the regime boundary is a property of the algorithm class, not the instance. Two results carry it - the coherent-feedback scaling collapse, and the mid-circuit Trotter witness - the two halves of one thesis.

Existing resource estimates price these algorithms as fixed circuits. The measurement bottleneck that dominates their asymptotic cost - and whether it can be removed - has not been costed as a scaling class.

FALQON (Lyapunov feedback control) is the concrete representative of the family whose cost this paper estimates: at each layer it measures the feedback observable i[H_d,H_p] and sets the next driver coefficient from it. Adaptive VQE, iterative phase estimation, and error-adaptive compilation are siblings in the same measurement-driven family.

Each measurement collapses the state, so the computation must restart from the beginning to produce the next layer. Cost therefore compounds with the number of iterations: the measure-and-restart structure is the object under study.

A broad family of quantum algorithms chooses each next operation from a measurement of the current state: the algorithm measures an observable, and the outcome sets the next unitary. These are the feedback, or adaptive, algorithms - introduced here by example before any instance is named formally.

A FALQON-based methodological development using mid-circuit measurements — how to run this class of measurement-driven iterative algorithm in a clever, coherent manner, and what that coherence gains — with the 30-site Fermi-Hubbard resource estimate as a separate supporting part.

N_free (link-witness-free) + N_generality: the paper's two-ordering heralding-bit witness generalised to a per-checkpoint family is derived to N_family_erradapt: error-adaptive compilation's coherent instance is the paper's own witness - flag written coherently by the LCU interferometer (C2 already built), consumed by a 1-bit-controlled correction (C3); per-checkpoint O(1) detect-and-ride, M^2->M collapse, for the quiet branch.

IPE round-k feedback angle omega_k linear in the measured bit register is derived to the classically-controlled rotation cascade IS the coherent inverse-QFT primitive (C1-C3 realised at zero collapse-cost; IPE the DEGENERATE class member).

N_generality: C1-C3 (link-generality) + ADAPT-VQE operator-pool gradient loop is derived to N_family_vqe: the L^2->L collapse applies to ADAPT-VQE's outer operator-selection loop, but consumption is an argmax (MAX, not linear) closing only at an O(P) pool prefactor.
N_layer: FALQON Lyapunov descent at 60 qubits, 500 layers (s9) is computed to N_converged: FALQON reaches a useful minimum at the scenario scale.
N_readings: the manuscript's emitted resource numbers (s4/s5/s6) is computed to N_ratios: SQL/COHERENT ~79,000 (T-count) / ~120,000 (wall-clock), 5 orders, across L=100..2000.

N_separation: the measure-and-restart L^2->L mechanism is derived to N_generality: the collapse is FALQON/Hubbard-INDEPENDENT — general to measurement-driven iterative algorithms with a coherently expressible feedback observable.
N_costs + N_quiet: per-layer resource ledger (s3) + the quiet symmetrised step is derived to N_free: witness rides inside the coherent feedback loop at ONE heralding bit, no restart.
N_flag: symmetrised (quiet) branch U_AB + U_BA is derived to N_quiet: quiet-branch step accurate to O(dt^3) vs bare O(dt^2) (odd commutator terms cancel).
N_flag: P(flag) + BCH expansion of U_BA^dag U_AB is derived to N_dt4: P(flag) ~ dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 to leading order (imaginary linear term suppressed by Re).
N_interf: two-ordering LCU interferometer (U_AB ctrl anc0, U_BA ctrl anc1, H+measure, s8) is derived to N_flag: P(flag) = (1/2)(1 - Re<U_BA^dag U_AB>), dark exactly when [A,B]=0.
N_sql_l2 + N_coherent_l: the two scalings side by side is derived to N_separation: SQL/COHERENT gap is a scaling-CLASS separation, ratio ~ L (widens), not a constant.
N_regwidth: 9-bit coherent register + register-controlled driver (s4) is derived to N_coherent_l: coherent circuit volume ~ L (single pass, constant control overhead).
N_prec: feedback precision eps_fb=0.01 (s4) is derived to N_regwidth: coherent register width b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb))+2 = 9 bits.
N_layer: FALQON layer structure (measure-and-restart, s2) is derived to N_sql_l2: SQL total layer-execution count ~ (L^2/2) * (1/eps_fb^2).
The physical qubit count is thus modestly layer-dependent, growing with code distance as the T-count rises, while the logical register remains fixed at 80 qubits across all three layer counts .

The commutator term that appears in and with opposite signs cancels in their sum, so the symmetrised step is accurate to rather than : detection and leading-error cancellation occur in the same measurement.
The readout does not collapse the system register the way the classical feedback loop does.
The comparison to QPE-class estimates is therefore not apples-to-apples in output type.

The per-layer cost is proportional to for the amplitude estimation plus a constant overhead for the register-controlled driver evolution and uncompute , giving a total layer-execution count that scales as rather than .
**Fully coherent feedback.** The structural bottleneck of the two preceding strategies is the classical loop: a measurement collapses the state, obligating a restart.

Amplitude estimation at Heisenberg scaling reduces the per-layer shot overhead but leaves the classical measurement-and-restart loop intact, so the quadratic layer structure persists and the wall clock falls only to 3.2 yr .

Abstract
FALQON on a Fault-Tolerant Machine: Resource Estimates for the 30-Site Fermi-Hubbard Model, and a Mid-Circuit Trotter-Error Witness
The Literature (citation map, 11 records)

Figures (the sweep tradeoff plot and the witness scaling plot, from the readings JSONs) are registered TODOs with none rendered; the large-error flag-branch fidelity 0.178 is present in the data but undiscussed in V1.

FALQON convergence at 60 logical qubits is unmeasured; the 500-layer assumption is the scenario's largest unverified input, and whether FALQON converges at this scale at all is an open empirical question.

The quantitative QPE comparison is deferred until the cited papers' numbers (`kivlichan2020improved`, `campbell2021early`, `babbush2018encoding`) enter through the door; V1 states only the qualitative scale claim.
The coherent register arithmetic is coarse: the 2x compute/uncompute overhead is a placeholder and the register-controlled driver costs ~b controlled evolutions; a proper costing is v2 work.
The commuting-group decomposition of i[H_d,H_p] for the Hubbard driver (measurement groups = 10, SCENARIO) needs the explicit construction; no source file provides it.

The TO-VERIFY constants bound how far the headline numbers should be trusted: the rotation count vs Kivlichan swap-network tables (`kivlichan2020improved`), the factory footprint and rate vs Litinski (`litinski2019game`), and the Ross-Selinger constant (`ross2016optimal`).
All 11 literature records were constructed without fetch access and are marked trust=unverified pending a door-check; their contents as cited are claims to be confirmed, not established fact.

`childs2012hamiltonian` anchors amplitude-estimation scaling in section 4 but is the adjacent LCU paper, not the canonical AE reference; replace with a Brassard-class citation once fetched through the door.
The section-9 rewrite removed two citation instances (`magann2022lyapunov` and `childs2021theory` at that location); both keys remain live elsewhere in the paper.

The weld pass wrote over its input file; the claim-lock diff ran against a deterministic rebuild of the pre-weld object — verified: 31 cite instances, 143 source tags, numeral multiset identical except one extra occurrence of the ledger-sourced 30 introduced by a seam bridge.
A misattribution in section 7 (SQL described as amplitude estimation; it is shot sampling) was fixed, and an uncited textbook name-drop neutralized to a generic textbook reference.
An overclaim in section 8 ("matches to within numerical precision at every step size") was false at dt=0.4 (15% higher-order gap; 0.06% by dt=0.025) and was restated as convergence.

A confabulated section-9 derivation (a Kraus-operator argument fusing the witness commutator with the feedback commutator, claiming the feedback register doubles as the witness at no gate cost) was unsupported by any source and rewritten to the supported insertion claim.
A drafter arithmetic error (sweep ratio stated as 50; the model's emitted value is 120) was caught mechanically by the numeral gate, not a judge.
Scope and limitations (drift ledger, FAL-CLM-201..214)

coherent_register_bits: Model parameter coherent_register_bits = 9 logical qubits. (Model tier DERIVED; b = log2(1/eps_fb)+2 bits hold A_k coherently.)

variance_per_group: Model parameter variance_per_group = 1. (Model tier SCENARIO; O(1) variance per normalized commuting group.)
commutator_measurement_groups: Model parameter commutator_measurement_groups = 10 groups. (Model tier SCENARIO; TO-VERIFY explicit grouping (see FAL-CLM-210).)
feedback_precision: Model parameter feedback_precision = 0.01. (Model tier SCENARIO; per-layer precision on A_k; swept 1e-1..1e-3.)
falqon_layers: Model parameter falqon_layers = 500 layers. (Model tier SCENARIO; convergence is THE open empirical question at this scale; swept 100-2000.)
T_per_rotation: Model parameter T_per_rotation = 100 T gates. (Model tier LITERATURE (ross-selinger-class TO-VERIFY); ~3 log2(1/eps) T per Rz.)

trotter_steps_per_layer: Model parameter trotter_steps_per_layer = 1 step. (Model tier SCENARIO; FALQON takes dt small, one 2nd-order step per layer.)

rotations_per_trotter_step: Model parameter rotations_per_trotter_step = 339 Rz. (Model tier DERIVED; 2nd-order step ~1.5x first-order count; swap-network locality assumed, TO-VERIFY vs Kivlichan-class counts at the door.)

hopping_terms: Model parameter hopping_terms = 98 terms. (Model tier DERIVED; 2 spins per bond.)

system_qubits: Model parameter system_qubits = 60 logical qubits. (Model tier DERIVED; one qubit per spin-orbital, Jordan-Wigner.)
lattice_y: Model parameter lattice_y = 5 sites. (Model tier SCENARIO.)
lattice_x: Model parameter lattice_x = 6 sites. (Model tier SCENARIO; 30-site 2D Hubbard as 6x5.)
Assumption registry (FAL-CLM-101..126)

Against QPE-class estimates the comparison is qualitative only: published Trotter-QPE, early-fault-tolerant, and qubitization estimates for Hubbard-class systems reach wall clocks into decades under standard T-factory throughput; the output types differ — coherent-FALQON delivers a prepared state, QPE an energy.

The witness inserts between FALQON layers at chosen checkpoints at the cost of one ancilla, roughly the depth of a second step, and a single computational-basis readout, without breaking the coherent run; the quiet outcome continues the run on the error-improved symmetrised step.
Large-error detection: at dt=0.8 the flag fires with probability 0.341 — appreciable errors are reliably detected rather than silently accepted.
Full evolution over T=2.0 in 40 steps: plain Trotter infidelity 1.18e-4 vs all-quiet witnessed 5.2e-6, with cumulative acceptance probability 0.9996.

Error cancellation: bare first-order Trotter infidelity scales as dt^3.951 while the quiet branch scales as dt^5.907 (~two orders of accuracy gained); at dt=0.4 the bare infidelity is 0.033 vs quiet-branch 4.6e-4.
Flag scaling: the log-log slope of P(flag) vs dt is 3.949 (theory: 4) across dt in {0.4, 0.2, 0.1, 0.05, 0.025}, converging onto dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 to better than one part in a thousand by dt=0.025.
Dark case (2-qubit TFIM at g=0, commuting layers): P(flag) = 0.0 and quiet-branch disturbance 0.0, exactly.

The two-ordering witness: control U_AB on ancilla 0 and U_BA on ancilla 1, Hadamard, measure; P(flag) = (1/2)(1 - Re <U_BA^dag U_AB>); dark exactly when [A,B]=0; flag probability scales as dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 to leading order; the quiet branch is the symmetrised step accurate to O(dt^3) (single-step analogue of multiproduct formulas); the flag branch isolates the error term.

T-count ratios at the headline point: SQL/AE = 640, AE/COHERENT = 120, SQL/COHERENT = 79,000 (~5 orders of magnitude); wall-clock SQL/COHERENT = 120,000 (~4 orders; throughput from the lower code distance partially offsets the T-count ratio).

Factory count is a pure throughput multiplier: at L=500, eps_fb=0.01 the COHERENT wall clock is 77.9 d at 1 factory (111,840 physical qubits), 187.0 h at 10 (165,840), 18.7 h at 100 (705,840); the total T-gate count is 5.342e9 in every case.

Feedback-precision sweep at L=500: SQL wall clock is 21.8 yr (eps 0.1), 2,502.1 yr (0.01), 282,500.0 yr (0.001) — eps^-2 scaling; COHERENT is 17.4 h, 187.0 h, 85.1 d — eps^-1 scaling; wall factors ~13,000 (SQL) vs ~120 (COHERENT) over the range.

COHERENT wall clock scales linearly in layer count at eps_fb=0.01: 33.8 h at 100 layers (d=19, 146,640 physical qubits), 187.0 h at 500 (d=21, 165,840), 819.1 h at 2000 (d=23, 186,960); the logical register stays fixed at 80 qubits.
The coherent register width is b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb)) + 2 = 9 bits at eps_fb = 0.01, with a factor-2 overhead for the register-controlled driver evolution and uncompute.

Fully coherent feedback writes A_k into a 9-bit ancilla register without measurement and applies the driver controlled on that register; no re-preparation is required, circuit volume scales as L not L^2; at L=500, eps_fb=0.01: 80 logical qubits, code distance 21, 165,840 physical qubits, 5.342e9 T gates, wall clock 187.0 h.

Amplitude estimation at Heisenberg scaling ((pi/2)/eps_fb controlled applications) reduces per-layer shot overhead but keeps the classical measure-and-restart loop, so the L^2 structure persists; at L=500, eps_fb=0.01: 69 logical qubits, code distance 25, 189,375 physical qubits, 6.656e11 T gates, wall clock 3.2 yr.

Under SQL (shot-sampling) feedback each layer re-executes all preceding layers (L^2/2 structure at 1/eps^2 shots per layer); at L=500, eps_fb=0.01 the run needs 61 logical qubits, code distance 31, 235,863 physical qubits, 4.2375e14 total T gates, wall clock 2,502.1 yr.
FALQON's feedback law beta_{k+1} = -<i[H_d,H_p]>_k realises Lyapunov monotonic descent of <H_p> layer to layer, with no classical optimizer consulting the parameter history.
Each Rz synthesises to 100 T gates at per-rotation synthesis error 1e-10 (Ross-Selinger class, ~3 log2(1/eps) T per rotation), so one Trotter step costs 33,900 T gates per layer.

One first-order Trotter step costs 226 Rz rotations; the second-order step is modelled at 1.5x, giving 339 Rz per step under a fermionic swap-network locality assumption, with one step per FALQON layer and a Trotter depth of 339 logical cycles per layer.
The 6x5 lattice has 49 nearest-neighbour edges, giving 98 hopping terms (2 spins per bond) and 30 on-site interaction terms.
The problem instance is a 30-site 2D Fermi-Hubbard model on a 6x5 lattice, two spin species per site, encoded on 60 logical qubits via Jordan-Wigner (one qubit per spin-orbital).

The fault-tolerant cost of FALQON's per-layer feedback measurement — how the measurement-and-restart structure and the required precision eps_fb scale against circuit depth — is the question a fault-tolerant resource analysis of FALQON must address.
Paper claims (evidence ledger FAL-CLM-001..022)

goals.essence: A FALQON-based methodological development using mid-circuit measurements — how to run this class of measurement-driven iterative algorithm in a clever, coherent manner, and what that coherence gains — with the 30-site Fermi-Hubbard resource estimate as a separate supporting part.

N_{\text{shots}}(\epsilon_{fb}) \sim \frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}^2}. \label{eq:sql-shots}
\text{(layer-executions per shot at layer } k) = k. \label{eq:sql-per-shot}
C_k = k \cdot N_{\text{shots}}(\epsilon_{fb}) = \frac{k}{\epsilon_{fb}^2}. \label{eq:sql-ck}
C_{\text{SQL}}(L,\epsilon_{fb}) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} C_k = \frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}^2}\sum_{k=1}^{L} k = \frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}^2}\cdot\frac{L(L+1)}{2}. \label{eq:sql-sum}
C_{\text{SQL}}(L,\epsilon_{fb}) \;\sim\; \frac{L^2}{2}\cdot\frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}^2} \qquad (L\to\infty). \label{eq:sql-result}
n = \left\lceil \log_2\!\frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}} \right\rceil. \label{eq:reg-n}
\log_2\frac{1}{0.01} = \log_2 100 = \frac{\ln 100}{\ln 2} = \frac{4.60517\ldots}{0.69315\ldots} = 6.6439\ldots \label{eq:reg-log}
n = \lceil 6.6439\ldots \rceil = 7 \text{ bits}. \label{eq:reg-n-val}
g(\delta) = \left\lceil \log_2\!\left(2 + \frac{1}{2\delta}\right)\right\rceil \label{eq:reg-guard}
g(1/4) = \left\lceil \log_2(2+2) \right\rceil = \lceil \log_2 4 \rceil = 2 \text{ bits}. \label{eq:reg-guard-val}
b = n + g = \left\lceil \log_2\frac{1}{\epsilon_{fb}}\right\rceil + 2 = 7 + 2 = 9 \text{ bits}. \label{eq:reg-total}
\text{(number of independent circuit executions)} = 1. \label{eq:coh-single}
c \;\approx\; 2, \label{eq:coh-c}
\text{(circuit cost of layer } k) = c, \quad \text{independent of } k. \label{eq:coh-perlayer}
C_{\text{coh}}(L) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} c = c\,L. \label{eq:coh-total}
C_{\text{SQL}}(L,\epsilon_{fb}) \sim \frac{L^2}{2\,\epsilon_{fb}^2}, \label{eq:sep-sql}
C_{\text{coh}}(L) = c\,L, \label{eq:sep-coh}
R(L) \;=\; \frac{C_{\text{SQL}}(L,\epsilon_{fb})}{C_{\text{coh}}(L)} \;=\; \frac{L^2/(2\epsilon_{fb}^2)}{c\,L} \;=\; \frac{L}{2c\,\epsilon_{fb}^2}. \label{eq:sep-ratio}
R(L) \to \infty \text{ as } L \to \infty, \quad \text{at any fixed } \epsilon_{fb} > 0. \label{eq:sep-divergence}
R(500) = \frac{500}{2\cdot 2\cdot (0.01)^2} = \frac{500}{4\times 10^{-4}} = 1.25\times 10^{6}. \label{eq:sep-numeric}
\theta_{k+1} = f(\hat{O}_k), \qquad \hat{O}_k \approx \langle O_k \rangle_{\text{state after round } k}. \label{eq:gen-setup}
N_{\text{shots}}(\epsilon) &\sim 1/\epsilon^2, \label{eq:gen-shots}\\ (\text{executions per shot at round } k) &= k. \label{eq:gen-reprep}
N_{\text{shots}}(\epsilon) &\sim 1/\epsilon^2, \label{eq:gen-shots}\\ (\text{executions per shot at round } k) &= k. \label{eq:gen-reprep}
C_{\text{measured}}(L,\epsilon) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} \frac{k}{\epsilon^2} = \frac{L(L+1)}{2\epsilon^2} \;\sim\; \frac{L^2}{2\epsilon^2}, \label{eq:gen-quadratic}
C_{\text{coherent}}(L) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} c \;=\; c\,L \;=\; O(L). \label{eq:gen-linear}
\lvert\psi_k\rangle = \prod_{j=1}^{k} e^{\theta_j A_{i_j}} \lvert\mathrm{ref}\rangle . \label{eq:vqe-ansatz}

g^{(k)}_i \;=\; \left.\frac{\partial}{\partial\theta}\right|_{\theta=0}\!\langle\psi_k\rvert e^{-\theta A_i} H\, e^{\theta A_i}\lvert\psi_k\rangle \;=\; \langle\psi_k\rvert [H,A_i]\lvert\psi_k\rangle , \label{eq:vqe-grad}
i^{\star} = \operatorname*{arg\,max}_{1\le i\le P} \lvert g^{(k)}_i\rvert, \qquad \text{append } e^{\theta_{i^\star} A_{i^\star}} . \label{eq:vqe-argmax}
C_{\text{measured}}(L,\epsilon) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} \frac{P\,k}{\epsilon^2} = \frac{P\,L(L+1)}{2\epsilon^2} \;\sim\; \frac{P\,L^2}{2\epsilon^2}, \label{eq:vqe-quadratic}
\textsc{sel}(\theta) = \sum_{i=1}^{P} \lvert i\rangle\!\langle i\rvert_{\mathrm{idx}} \otimes e^{\theta A_i}, \label{eq:vqe-select}
C_{\text{coherent}}(L) = \sum_{k=1}^{L} c_P = c_P\,L = O(P\,L), \quad c_P = O(P b), \label{eq:vqe-linear}
\frac{C_{\text{measured}}}{C_{\text{coherent}}} \;\sim\; \frac{P\,L^2/(2\epsilon^2)}{c_P\,L} \;=\; \frac{L}{2 c'\epsilon^2}, \ c' = c_P/P = O(b), \label{eq:vqe-ratio}
\omega_k = -2\pi\,(0.\,0\,b_{k-1}b_{k-2}\cdots b_1) = -2\pi\sum_{\ell=1}^{k-1} b_\ell\, 2^{-(k-\ell+1)} . \label{eq:ipe-feedback}

C_{\text{measured}}(L,\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}) = \sum_{k=1}^{L}\frac{k}{\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}^2} = \frac{L(L+1)}{2\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}^2}\;\sim\;\frac{L^2}{2\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}^2}, \label{eq:ipe-quadratic}
R_z(\omega_k)\big|_{b_\ell\ \mathrm{classical}} \;\longmapsto\; \prod_{\ell=1}^{k-1} \Lambda_\ell\!\left(R_z\!\big(-2\pi\,2^{-(k-\ell+1)}\big)\right), \label{eq:ipe-controlled}

\begin{split} C_{\text{coherent}}(L) &= \sum_{k=1}^{L} c \;=\; cL \;=\; O(L),\\ \frac{C_{\text{measured}}}{C_{\text{coherent}}} &\sim \frac{L}{2c\,\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}^2}\ \xrightarrow[L\to\infty]{}\ \infty . \end{split} \label{eq:ipe-lin

\begin{split} P(\mathrm{flag}) &= \tfrac12\!\left(1-\mathrm{Re}\langle U_{BA}^\dagger U_{AB}\rangle\right) \\ &= \frac{dt^4}{4}\langle K^\dagger K\rangle + O(dt^5),\qquad K=[A,B], \end{split} \label{eq:ea-flag}

C_{\text{measured}}(M,\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}) = \sum_{k=1}^{M}\frac{k}{\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}^2} = \frac{M(M+1)}{2\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}^2}\;\sim\;\frac{M^2}{2\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}^2}. \label{eq:ea-quadratic}
V_k = \lvert 0\rangle\!\langle 0\rvert_{\mathrm{flag}}\otimes \mathbb{1} \;+\; \lvert 1\rangle\!\langle 1\rvert_{\mathrm{flag}}\otimes \mathcal{R}_k, \label{eq:ea-controlled}

\begin{split} c_{\text{chk}} &= \underbrace{1}_{\text{ancilla (shared)}} + \underbrace{O(1)\ \text{Trotter-step depth}}_{\text{interferometer}} \\ &\qquad {}+ \underbrace{[\,f_k=1\,]\,\lVert\mathcal{R}_k\rVert}_{\text{correction, loud only}

\begin{split} C_{\text{coherent}}(M) &= \sum_{k=1}^{M} c_{\text{chk}} = O(M),\\ \frac{C_{\text{measured}}}{C_{\text{coherent}}} &\sim\frac{M}{2\epsilon_{\mathrm{fb}}^2}\ \xrightarrow[M\to\infty]{}\ \infty . \end{split} \label{eq:ea-linear}

\begin{split} P(\mathrm{flag}) &= \frac{1}{4}\Bigl[2 - 2\,\mathrm{Re}\langle U_{BA}^\dagger U_{AB}\rangle\Bigr] \\ &= \frac{1}{2}\Bigl(1 - \mathrm{Re}\langle U_{BA}^\dagger U_{AB}\rangle\Bigr). \label{eq:pflag} \end{split}
U_{BA}^\dagger U_{AB} = \exp\!\Bigl(-dt^2\,[A,B] + O(dt^3)\Bigr). \label{eq:relgen}
\mathrm{Re}\langle U_{BA}^\dagger U_{AB}\rangle = 1 - \frac{dt^4}{2}\langle K^\dagger K\rangle + O(dt^5). \label{eq:reoverlap}

\begin{split} P(\mathrm{flag}) &= \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{dt^4}{2}\langle K^\dagger K\rangle + O(dt^5)\right) \\ &= \frac{dt^4}{4}\langle K^\dagger K\rangle + O(dt^5), \qquad K = [A,B]. \label{eq:pflagdt4} \end{split}
\begin{split} U_{AB} &= e^{-iAdt}e^{-iBdt} \\ &= \mathbb{1} - i(A+B)dt \\ &\quad {}- \frac{dt^2}{2}\bigl(A^2+B^2+2AB\bigr) + O(dt^3). \label{eq:UABexp} \end{split}

\begin{split} e^{-i(A+B)dt} &= \mathbb{1} - i(A+B)dt - \frac{dt^2}{2}(A+B)^2 + O(dt^3) \\ &= \mathbb{1} - i(A+B)dt \\ &\quad {}- \frac{dt^2}{2}\bigl(A^2+B^2+AB+BA\bigr) + O(dt^3). \label{eq:exactexp} \end{split}
\begin{split} U_{AB} - e^{-i(A+B)dt} &= -\frac{dt^2}{2}\bigl(2AB - AB - BA\bigr) + O(dt^3) \\ &= -\frac{dt^2}{2}[A,B] + O(dt^3). \label{eq:bareerr} \end{split}
\begin{split} U_{BA} - e^{-i(A+B)dt} &= -\frac{dt^2}{2}[B,A] + O(dt^3) \\ &= +\frac{dt^2}{2}[A,B] + O(dt^3). \label{eq:baerr} \end{split}
\begin{split} \frac{U_{AB}+U_{BA}}{2} &- e^{-i(A+B)dt} \\ &= \frac{1}{2}\left[-\frac{dt^2}{2}[A,B] + \frac{dt^2}{2}[A,B]\right] + O(dt^3) \\ &= O(dt^3). \label{eq:symerr} \end{split}
\text{(ancilla)} = 1, \; \text{(rotation depth)} \approx d_{\text{layer}}, \; \text{(readout)} = 1, \label{eq:wf-ledger}
\Delta(\text{ancilla count}) = 0 \label{eq:wf-anc}
\text{overhead fraction} = \frac{d_{\text{layer}}}{m \cdot d_{\text{layer}}} = \frac{1}{m}. \label{eq:wf-overhead}
\begin{split} C_{\text{coherent+witness}}(L) &= c\,L + \frac{L}{m}\,d_{\text{layer}} \\ &= \left(c + \frac{d_{\text{layer}}}{m}\right) L = c' L = O(L). \end{split} \label{eq:wf-total}
Paper claims (evidence ledger FAL-CLM-001..022)

The fault-tolerant cost of FALQON's per-layer feedback measurement — how the measurement-and-restart structure and the required precision eps_fb scale against circuit depth — is the question a fault-tolerant resource analysis of FALQON must address.
The problem instance is a 30-site 2D Fermi-Hubbard model on a 6x5 lattice, two spin species per site, encoded on 60 logical qubits via Jordan-Wigner (one qubit per spin-orbital).
The 6x5 lattice has 49 nearest-neighbour edges, giving 98 hopping terms (2 spins per bond) and 30 on-site interaction terms.

One first-order Trotter step costs 226 Rz rotations; the second-order step is modelled at 1.5x, giving 339 Rz per step under a fermionic swap-network locality assumption, with one step per FALQON layer and a Trotter depth of 339 logical cycles per layer.
Each Rz synthesises to 100 T gates at per-rotation synthesis error 1e-10 (Ross-Selinger class, ~3 log2(1/eps) T per rotation), so one Trotter step costs 33,900 T gates per layer.
FALQON's feedback law beta_{k+1} = -<i[H_d,H_p]>_k realises Lyapunov monotonic descent of <H_p> layer to layer, with no classical optimizer consulting the parameter history.

Under SQL (shot-sampling) feedback each layer re-executes all preceding layers (L^2/2 structure at 1/eps^2 shots per layer); at L=500, eps_fb=0.01 the run needs 61 logical qubits, code distance 31, 235,863 physical qubits, 4.2375e14 total T gates, wall clock 2,502.1 yr.

Amplitude estimation at Heisenberg scaling ((pi/2)/eps_fb controlled applications) reduces per-layer shot overhead but keeps the classical measure-and-restart loop, so the L^2 structure persists; at L=500, eps_fb=0.01: 69 logical qubits, code distance 25, 189,375 physical qubits, 6.656e11 T gates, wall clock 3.2 yr.

Fully coherent feedback writes A_k into a 9-bit ancilla register without measurement and applies the driver controlled on that register; no re-preparation is required, circuit volume scales as L not L^2; at L=500, eps_fb=0.01: 80 logical qubits, code distance 21, 165,840 physical qubits, 5.342e9 T gates, wall clock 187.0 h.
The coherent register width is b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb)) + 2 = 9 bits at eps_fb = 0.01, with a factor-2 overhead for the register-controlled driver evolution and uncompute.

COHERENT wall clock scales linearly in layer count at eps_fb=0.01: 33.8 h at 100 layers (d=19, 146,640 physical qubits), 187.0 h at 500 (d=21, 165,840), 819.1 h at 2000 (d=23, 186,960); the logical register stays fixed at 80 qubits.

Feedback-precision sweep at L=500: SQL wall clock is 21.8 yr (eps 0.1), 2,502.1 yr (0.01), 282,500.0 yr (0.001) — eps^-2 scaling; COHERENT is 17.4 h, 187.0 h, 85.1 d — eps^-1 scaling; wall factors ~13,000 (SQL) vs ~120 (COHERENT) over the range.

Factory count is a pure throughput multiplier: at L=500, eps_fb=0.01 the COHERENT wall clock is 77.9 d at 1 factory (111,840 physical qubits), 187.0 h at 10 (165,840), 18.7 h at 100 (705,840); the total T-gate count is 5.342e9 in every case.

T-count ratios at the headline point: SQL/AE = 640, AE/COHERENT = 120, SQL/COHERENT = 79,000 (~5 orders of magnitude); wall-clock SQL/COHERENT = 120,000 (~4 orders; throughput from the lower code distance partially offsets the T-count ratio).

The two-ordering witness: control U_AB on ancilla 0 and U_BA on ancilla 1, Hadamard, measure; P(flag) = (1/2)(1 - Re <U_BA^dag U_AB>); dark exactly when [A,B]=0; flag probability scales as dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 to leading order; the quiet branch is the symmetrised step accurate to O(dt^3) (single-step analogue of multiproduct formulas); the flag branch isolates the error term.
Dark case (2-qubit TFIM at g=0, commuting layers): P(flag) = 0.0 and quiet-branch disturbance 0.0, exactly.
Flag scaling: the log-log slope of P(flag) vs dt is 3.949 (theory: 4) across dt in {0.4, 0.2, 0.1, 0.05, 0.025}, converging onto dt^4 <K^dag K>/4 to better than one part in a thousand by dt=0.025.

Error cancellation: bare first-order Trotter infidelity scales as dt^3.951 while the quiet branch scales as dt^5.907 (~two orders of accuracy gained); at dt=0.4 the bare infidelity is 0.033 vs quiet-branch 4.6e-4.
Full evolution over T=2.0 in 40 steps: plain Trotter infidelity 1.18e-4 vs all-quiet witnessed 5.2e-6, with cumulative acceptance probability 0.9996.
Large-error detection: at dt=0.8 the flag fires with probability 0.341 — appreciable errors are reliably detected rather than silently accepted.

The witness inserts between FALQON layers at chosen checkpoints at the cost of one ancilla, roughly the depth of a second step, and a single computational-basis readout, without breaking the coherent run; the quiet outcome continues the run on the error-improved symmetrised step.

Against QPE-class estimates the comparison is qualitative only: published Trotter-QPE, early-fault-tolerant, and qubitization estimates for Hubbard-class systems reach wall clocks into decades under standard T-factory throughput; the output types differ — coherent-FALQON delivers a prepared state, QPE an energy.
Assumption registry (FAL-CLM-101..126)
lattice_x: Model parameter lattice_x = 6 sites. (Model tier SCENARIO; 30-site 2D Hubbard as 6x5.)
lattice_y: Model parameter lattice_y = 5 sites. (Model tier SCENARIO.)
system_qubits: Model parameter system_qubits = 60 logical qubits. (Model tier DERIVED; one qubit per spin-orbital, Jordan-Wigner.)
lattice_edges: Model parameter lattice_edges = 49 edges. (Model tier DERIVED; nearest-neighbour bonds of the 6x5 lattice.)
hopping_terms: Model parameter hopping_terms = 98 terms. (Model tier DERIVED; 2 spins per bond.)
onsite_terms: Model parameter onsite_terms = 30 terms. (Model tier DERIVED; U n_up n_down per site.)
first_order_rotations: Model parameter first_order_rotations = 226 Rz. (Model tier DERIVED; one rotation per hopping pair plus one per on-site term.)

rotations_per_trotter_step: Model parameter rotations_per_trotter_step = 339 Rz. (Model tier DERIVED; 2nd-order step ~1.5x first-order count; swap-network locality assumed, TO-VERIFY vs Kivlichan-class counts at the door.)
trotter_steps_per_layer: Model parameter trotter_steps_per_layer = 1 step. (Model tier SCENARIO; FALQON takes dt small, one 2nd-order step per layer.)
rotation_synthesis_error: Model parameter rotation_synthesis_error = 1e-10. (Model tier SCENARIO; per-rotation synthesis target, budget-consistent with total rotation count.)
T_per_rotation: Model parameter T_per_rotation = 100 T gates. (Model tier LITERATURE (ross-selinger-class TO-VERIFY); ~3 log2(1/eps) T per Rz.)
falqon_layers: Model parameter falqon_layers = 500 layers. (Model tier SCENARIO; convergence is THE open empirical question at this scale; swept 100-2000.)
feedback_precision: Model parameter feedback_precision = 0.01. (Model tier SCENARIO; per-layer precision on A_k; swept 1e-1..1e-3.)
commutator_measurement_groups: Model parameter commutator_measurement_groups = 10 groups. (Model tier SCENARIO; TO-VERIFY explicit grouping (see FAL-CLM-210).)
variance_per_group: Model parameter variance_per_group = 1. (Model tier SCENARIO; O(1) variance per normalized commuting group.)
amplitude_estimation_constant: Model parameter amplitude_estimation_constant = 1.5708. (Model tier TEXTBOOK; ~(pi/2)/eps controlled applications, Heisenberg-limited.)
coherent_register_bits: Model parameter coherent_register_bits = 9 logical qubits. (Model tier DERIVED; b = log2(1/eps_fb)+2 bits hold A_k coherently.)
coherent_ctrl_overhead: Model parameter coherent_ctrl_overhead = 2. (Model tier SCENARIO; register-controlled driver evolution + uncompute vs bare layer (see FAL-CLM-211).)

Scope and limitations (drift ledger, FAL-CLM-201..214)
A drafter arithmetic error (sweep ratio stated as 50; the model's emitted value is 120) was caught mechanically by the numeral gate, not a judge.

A confabulated section-9 derivation (a Kraus-operator argument fusing the witness commutator with the feedback commutator, claiming the feedback register doubles as the witness at no gate cost) was unsupported by any source and rewritten to the supported insertion claim.
An overclaim in section 8 ("matches to within numerical precision at every step size") was false at dt=0.4 (15% higher-order gap; 0.06% by dt=0.025) and was restated as convergence.
A misattribution in section 7 (SQL described as amplitude estimation; it is shot sampling) was fixed, and an uncited textbook name-drop neutralized to a generic textbook reference.

The weld pass wrote over its input file; the claim-lock diff ran against a deterministic rebuild of the pre-weld object — verified: 31 cite instances, 143 source tags, numeral multiset identical except one extra occurrence of the ledger-sourced 30 introduced by a seam bridge.
The section-9 rewrite removed two citation instances (`magann2022lyapunov` and `childs2021theory` at that location); both keys remain live elsewhere in the paper.

`childs2012hamiltonian` anchors amplitude-estimation scaling in section 4 but is the adjacent LCU paper, not the canonical AE reference; replace with a Brassard-class citation once fetched through the door.
All 11 literature records were constructed without fetch access and are marked trust=unverified pending a door-check; their contents as cited are claims to be confirmed, not established fact.

The TO-VERIFY constants bound how far the headline numbers should be trusted: the rotation count vs Kivlichan swap-network tables (`kivlichan2020improved`), the factory footprint and rate vs Litinski (`litinski2019game`), and the Ross-Selinger constant (`ross2016optimal`).
The commuting-group decomposition of i[H_d,H_p] for the Hubbard driver (measurement groups = 10, SCENARIO) needs the explicit construction; no source file provides it.
The coherent register arithmetic is coarse: the 2x compute/uncompute overhead is a placeholder and the register-controlled driver costs ~b controlled evolutions; a proper costing is v2 work.

The quantitative QPE comparison is deferred until the cited papers' numbers (`kivlichan2020improved`, `campbell2021early`, `babbush2018encoding`) enter through the door; V1 states only the qualitative scale claim.

FALQON convergence at 60 logical qubits is unmeasured; the 500-layer assumption is the scenario's largest unverified input, and whether FALQON converges at this scale at all is an open empirical question.

Figures (the sweep tradeoff plot and the witness scaling plot, from the readings JSONs) are registered TODOs with none rendered; the large-error flag-branch fidelity 0.178 is present in the data but undiscussed in V1.

**Fully coherent feedback.** The structural bottleneck of the two preceding strategies is the classical loop: a measurement collapses the state, obligating a restart.

The per-layer cost is proportional to for the amplitude estimation plus a constant overhead for the register-controlled driver evolution and uncompute , giving a total layer-execution count that scales as rather than .
The comparison to QPE-class estimates is therefore not apples-to-apples in output type.
The readout does not collapse the system register the way the classical feedback loop does.

The physical qubit count is thus modestly layer-dependent, growing with code distance as the T-count rises, while the logical register remains fixed at 80 qubits across all three layer counts .

IPE round-k feedback angle omega_k linear in the measured bit register is derived to the classically-controlled rotation cascade IS the coherent inverse-QFT primitive (C1-C3 realised at zero collapse-cost; IPE the DEGENERATE class member).

N_free (link-witness-free) + N_generality: the paper's two-ordering heralding-bit witness generalised to a per-checkpoint family is derived to N_family_erradapt: error-adaptive compilation's coherent instance is the paper's own witness - flag written coherently by the LCU interferometer (C2 already built), consumed by a 1-bit-controlled correction (C3); per-checkpoint O(1) detect-and-ride, M^2->M collapse, for the quiet branch.

A broad family of quantum algorithms chooses each next operation from a measurement of the current state: the algorithm measures an observable, and the outcome sets the next unitary. These are the feedback, or adaptive, algorithms - introduced here by example before any instance is named formally.

Each measurement collapses the state, so the computation must restart from the beginning to produce the next layer. Cost therefore compounds with the number of iterations: the measure-and-restart structure is the object under study.

FALQON (Lyapunov feedback control) is the concrete representative of the family whose cost this paper estimates: at each layer it measures the feedback observable i[H_d,H_p] and sets the next driver coefficient from it. Adaptive VQE, iterative phase estimation, and error-adaptive compilation are siblings in the same measurement-driven family.

Existing resource estimates price these algorithms as fixed circuits. The measurement bottleneck that dominates their asymptotic cost - and whether it can be removed - has not been costed as a scaling class.

This work shows that confining measurement to one heralding bit moves the whole class from the measurement-dominated regime (millennia) to the coherence-dominated one (hours): circuit volume collapses L^2 to L, and the regime boundary is a property of the algorithm class, not the instance. Two results carry it - the coherent-feedback scaling collapse, and the mid-circuit Trotter witness - the two halves of one thesis.

The argument proceeds: fix the instance and its assumptions; give FALQON measured quantity; set the Trotter step; compare the three evaluation routes; report the estimates; identify the levers; position against the QPE-class literature; present the mid-circuit witness; state the honest limits.

FALQON discretised feedback law sets beta_{k+1} = -<i[H_d,H_p]>_k: a Lyapunov control that drives the energy monotonically downward because the sign of the update opposes the gradient, guaranteeing the energy decreases or stays flat from layer to layer. Each layer must measure the commutator expectation <i[H_d,H_p]>.

Fault-tolerant cost here means: logical versus physical qubits, T-gate count, and wall-clock time via a surface-code layer (physical error rate against threshold, code-cycle time, magic-state distillation). These are the units every later number is quoted in.

The feedback estimate carries a precision eps_fb, and the coherent register width is b = ceil(log2(1/eps_fb)) + 2 bits, forced by that precision. Circuit depth will be shown to scale against this precision - not against a convergence criterion.

above.essence: \begin{split} U_{AB} - e^{-i(A+B)dt} &= -\frac{dt^2}{2}\bigl(2AB - AB - BA\bigr) + O(dt^3) \\ &= -\frac{dt^2}{2}[A,B] + O(dt^3). \label{eq:bareerr} \end{split}

below.essence: \begin{split} P(\mathrm{flag}) &= \frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{dt^4}{2}\langle K^\dagger K\rangle + O(dt^5)\right) \\ &= \frac{dt^4}{4}\langle K^\dagger K\rangle + O(dt^5), \qquad K = [A,B]. \label{eq:pflagdt4} \end{split}

The fault-tolerant cost of FALQON's per-layer feedback measurement — how the measurement-and-restart structure and the required precision eps_fb scale against circuit depth — is the question a fault-tolerant resource analysis of FALQON must address.

lattice_y: Model parameter lattice_y = 5 sites. (Model tier SCENARIO.)
